achievements: Complete infrastructure migration from Digital Ocean to AWS
Full backend overhaul improving performance and security
project.description: Migrated Chanced.com from Digital Ocean to AWS and performed a complete overhaul of their PHP Laravel backend. The project modernized the hosting infrastructure and improved application performance and reliability.
project.title: Digital Ocean to AWS Migration & Backend Overhaul
project.technicalApproach: Migrated hosting from Digital Ocean to AWS. Overhauled the entire PHP Laravel backend for improved performance, security, and maintainability. Leveraged AWS services for hosting, storage, and compute.

achievements: Secure, localized trading platform with CMS-driven content
Automated CI/CD pipeline with Azure Pipelines
Global performance optimization with Cloudflare CDN
project.description: Built a secure cryptocurrency trading platform frontend with dynamic, localized content. The platform features server-side rendering, CMS-driven content management, and CDN optimization for global performance.
project.title: Secure Crypto Trading Platform
project.technicalApproach: Developed the frontend using Next.js with Strapi CMS for dynamic content management. Set up CI/CD with Azure Pipelines for automated deployments. Applied Cloudflare optimizations for global performance and security.

achievements: High-performance web application with server-side rendering
project.description: Created a performant front-end for an Amplify-hosted web application using Next.js. The platform features server-side rendering for optimal performance and is deployed on AWS Amplify for streamlined hosting and CI/CD.
project.title: Performant Web Application on AWS Amplify
project.technicalApproach: Built the application with Next.js and TypeScript, leveraging server-side rendering for performance. Deployed on AWS Amplify with integrated hosting and continuous deployment pipelines.

achievements: Full multitenant HR platform supporting multiple organizations
Integrated video interviewing and resume parsing capabilities
Streamlined candidate workflows from application to hire
project.description: Built a multitenant human resources application that integrates video interviewing, resume parsing, and candidate workflows. The platform supports multiple organizations on a single deployment with tenant isolation, enabling end-to-end recruitment process automation.
project.title: Multitenant HR Platform with Video Interviewing
project.technicalApproach: Developed a React-based frontend with AWS serverless backend. Integrated video interviewing capabilities, AI-powered resume parsing, and candidate workflow management. Built with multi-tenancy support for organizational isolation.

achievements: Automated translation of safety manuals from German to multiple languages
Eliminated manual translation and formatting workflows
Maintained document formatting integrity across languages
project.description: Built an automated transcription and translation system for safety manuals, converting German-language PDF articles into every required country-specific language. The platform ingests PDFs, extracts text, translates content, and regenerates formatted PDF output.
project.title: Multilingual Safety Manual Translation Platform
project.technicalApproach: Used Amazon Textract to extract text from PDF safety manuals. Integrated Amazon Translate for multilingual conversion. Built an automated pipeline to reconvert translated text back into formatted PDF documents.

recommendations: 45 critical gap(s) identified. Consider teaming arrangements or subcontractors with relevant experience.
Overall past performance coverage is below 70%. This may significantly impact win probability.
criticalGaps: Implement a more functional, robust, and advanced email marketing and campaign management platform to replace Adobe Campaign Standard
Provide implementation services including hands-on keyboard support, seamless integration, data migration, IP warming, testing, and overall platform setup
Support configuration of multiple instances and migration of large database, tables, workflows, and audience profiles
Provide dedicated implementation team (in-house or third-party) for implementation services
Provide user training and support after implementation

Platform must support email marketing communications to millions of registered players including game information, promotions, winner news, responsible gaming tips, retailer surveys, and other program communications
SaaS must be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, excluding agreed-upon maintenance downtime
SaaS monthly availability must average at least 99.9% excluding agreed-upon maintenance downtime
Data must be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, excluding agreed-upon maintenance downtime with 99.9% monthly availability
Provide advance written notice of any major upgrades or changes that will affect SaaS availability

Provide customer service strategy including SLAs, dedicated account team or single point of contact for real-time support, hours of coverage, platform uptime guarantees, and outage communication procedures
Provide monitoring alerts with steps to ensure they are acted upon and resolved immediately per SLA
Provide training sessions and resources for users to effectively use the platform
All sensitive and confidential electronic Lottery Data in transit or at rest must be encrypted in compliance with FIPS 140-3 Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
Contractor must align with ISO/IEC 27001, ISO/IEC 27002, NIST SP 800-53, FIPS 199, and FIPS 140-3 standards
Provide a Security Plan within 10 days after contract execution identifying administrative, physical, and technical security controls, and update annually

Maintain Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plan covering disaster management strategy, staff responsibilities, prioritization for recovery of critical systems, and operational recovery procedures
Maintain data backup and recovery processes for all critical Lottery Data with defined Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO)
All Lottery Data maintained by Contractor must be physically located within the continental United States
Remote access to Lottery Data from outside the United States is prohibited unless approved in advance in writing by the Lottery
Notify Lottery Contract Manager, Deputy Director of SLED, and Information Security Office within two hours after discovery of an Information Security Incident
Disposal of sensitive and confidential Lottery Data must meet ISO/IEC 27001 and NIST 800-88 minimum requirements
Upon contract expiration or termination, all Lottery Data must be returned or destroyed beyond recovery within 90 days
Maintain security audit logs of any access or other activities associated with Lottery information
Implement separation of duties, least privilege, and need-to-know access controls as part of the Security Plan
Contractor and personnel must submit to background investigations including fingerprinting through DOJ and FBI
Contractor personnel must not be assigned to work on the Lottery Contract if convicted of a felony, gambling-related offense, or crime involving dishonesty
Agree to Lottery Special Terms and Conditions (Exhibit C) without deviations
Agree to Information Technology Terms and Conditions (Exhibit D)
Submit Responsibility Certification confirming not presently debarred, suspended, or proposed for debarment
Submit Proposer Certification (Attachment 5) acknowledging all certification terms
Joint proposals are not permitted; award will be to a single primary proposer
Proposer's response constitutes an irrevocable offer for 180 calendar days following the proposal due date
Disclose any use of Generative AI per GenAI Impact Assessment Form (Attachment 12) if applicable
Contractor must comply with Drug-Free Workplace Act of 1990
Contractor must comply with Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) of 1990
Contractor must be qualified to do business in California and in good standing with the California Secretary of State
Maintain all Contract-related financial and accounting records for four years after contract expiration and make available for Lottery audit
Proposer must have a minimum of two (2) and not more than four (4) contract/implementations demonstrating relevant experience within the last 10 years completed in the United States
Designate a single point of contact for background processing
Provide references from end users (not other contractors) for each contract/implementation cited in qualifications
Document all assumptions about the SOW, responsibilities, and any issues relevant to proposal submission and ability to perform work for proposed cost
No cost information may be included in the body of the proposal; cost must only appear in Volume 3
All proposed costs must be all-inclusive of direct and indirect costs for all activities, labor, materials, tools, equipment, and travel
Cost evaluation includes first two base years and three optional years for evaluation purposes
